title: Lagochilopsis hirtus
taxonomy: Lagochilopsis hirtus is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States, specifically in the states of Arizona and New Mexico. The plant has a classification that is often debated among botanists, with some sources grouping it with the genus Lagochilus. Further research is needed to confirm the accurate classification of Lagochilopsis hirtus. However, it is generally recognized as a member of the family Thymelaeaceae.

intro: Lagochilopsis hirtus, also known as the hairy lagochilopsis, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage.
